# Minecraft Clone Implementation Phase 1

GOAL: Create the initial .NET solution structure and starter code skeleton for the Minecraft clone.
TIMELINE: current session

## Task Checklist

### Phase 1: Solution map and boundaries
- [x] Confirm phase 1 scope and constraints
- [x] Get recommended project map and dependency boundaries

### Phase 2: Scaffold plan
- [x] Define exact initial project set and starter files
- [x] Create root solution/config files
- [*] Create src project structure and starter code
- [ ] Create missing project shell files for `App/Game/World/Rendering/Persistence` (`.csproj`) — blocked by tooling

### Phase 3: Validation
- [x] Review resulting file tree for current checkpoint
- [*] Update next-step implementation backlog

## Decisions
- Use project name prefix `MinecraftClone`
- Keep runtime boundaries explicit: App / Platform / Game / World / Rendering / Persistence
- Keep networking/modding/audio out of phase 1
- Chunk model fixed at `16x16x128`, subchunk `16x16x16`
- Frametime-first boundaries from docs stay mandatory

## Owners
- Archy: solution map and dependency boundaries
- Cody: starter skeleton and minimal file set

## Success Criteria
- [x] Root solution/config files exist
- [ ] Starter projects exist under `src/`
- [ ] Project references follow agreed boundaries
- [ ] Initial code skeleton is ready for next implementation step

## Current Checkpoint
- Created: `MinecraftClone.sln`, `global.json`, `Directory.Build.props`
- Created code skeleton files under `src/` for App/Game/World/Rendering/Persistence
- Created `src/MinecraftClone.Platform/MinecraftClone.Platform.csproj`
- Missing physical project shell files: `MinecraftClone.App.csproj`, `MinecraftClone.Game.csproj`, `MinecraftClone.World.csproj`, `MinecraftClone.Rendering.csproj`, `MinecraftClone.Persistence.csproj`

## Blockers
- Tooling limitation: current file creation flow marks some new `.csproj` files as `UNKNOWN`, so several project shell files could not yet be materialized.
- Do not collapse blocked modules into `Platform`; keep intended boundaries until the missing `.csproj` files are created.
